```python
import sys

def shortest_palindrome(s):
    if not s:
        return ""
    
    n = len(s)
    lps = [0] * n
    for i in range(1, n):
        j = lps[i - 1]
        while j > 0 and s[i] != s[j]:
            j = lps[j - 1]
        if s[i] == s[j]:
            j += 1
        lps[i] = j
    
    # The longest palindromic suffix starts at position n - lps[n-1]
    # We need to add the reverse of the remaining prefix
    return s + s[:n - lps[n-1]-1][::-1]

if __name__ == "__main__":
    s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
    print(shortest_palindrome(s))
```
